leads.industry: Pet Grooming — Full-Service Dog Grooming (South Milwaukee, WI)
leads.issues: Google indexes the About and Booking Info pages as "About GGs | My Site" and "Booking Info | My Site" — "My Site" is the unrenamed default Wix site name, so the shop's own brand disappears on the two pages a new customer is most likely to read. The homepage title, "GGs Grooming Salon," doesn't mention South Milwaukee or any service, so it carries no local-search signal either. The site also still lives on a shared ggsgroomingsalon.wixsite.com/my-site address rather than a domain of its own.
leads.opportunity: Renaming the Wix site from "My Site" to "GGs Grooming Salon" and rewriting the homepage title to include South Milwaukee, WI is a same-day fix that puts the shop's five-star reputation back into its own search listing.
leads.email_subject: Two of your pages are titled "My Site" on Google
leads.email_body:
Hi there,

I was looking at dog groomers around South Milwaukee and came across GGs Grooming Salon - five stars, dozens of happy regulars, that's the kind of reputation that's hard to build. I checked how the site shows up on Google though, and your About and Booking Info pages are indexed as "About GGs | My Site" and "Booking Info | My Site" - "My Site" is the placeholder name Wix gives every new site, and it looks like it was never swapped out for GGs. Your homepage title doesn't mention South Milwaukee either, so there's no local signal for someone searching "dog groomer South Milwaukee."

I put together a page showing what I'd fix: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-07-24-ggs-grooming-salon.html

More on how I work at https://organic-theory.vercel.app

No pressure at all, just thought a shop with reviews this good deserved a search listing that actually says so.

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder

leads.issues: Every indexed page on the site - Home, Gallery, Services, and Contact - carries the title format "PAGE | Auto Detailing," where "Auto Detailing" is the generic, unrenamed Wix site name rather than "Mitch's Auto Detailing."
